paper_reference: Table 4.3 - 4-AMPY ligand passivation study on PbS CQD photodetectors

j_btb_exclusion.rationale: J_BTB (band-to-band tunneling) requires high doping concentrations and extremely narrow depletion regions (typically <10 nm), conditions not satisfied in PbS CQD photodetectors due to their moderate doping levels (~10^16 cm^-3) and relatively thick active layers (~200-400 nm). The CQD film's granular structure and organic ligand matrix further preclude the coherent band bending required for direct band-to-band tunneling. Including an unphysical J_BTB component would introduce an extra free parameter that degrades the physical interpretability of J0, A, and J_TAT parameters without improving fit quality — a classic case of overfitting.

parameter_naming_note: In the Python code, the TAT denominator exponent parameter is 'c' (lowercase). In the academic paper Table 4.3 and all generated reports, it is labeled 'C' (uppercase) to match publication convention.
